mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5d returns remain positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60d returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate profit expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20d returns during easing periods, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 245 index threshold forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5d returns in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20d returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly expands the economic pie and corporate earnings base, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows zero predictive power for 60d returns across all macro regimes, with cumulative ICIR below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in retail sales signals immediate consumer demand destruction, forcing earnings downgrades and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns remain positive in >60% of periods following the retail sales threshold breach, or signal Sharpe ratio is negative.
